post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 157: Cop Shop
Rules
  • Cop Code of Conduct
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.9: In your first post, post something that would usually give you a warning in a usual game, but is not actually breaking any rules in this game. If I do not find that you have followed this rule correctly, you will not be able to perform any actions Night 1 (if you have any to perform).
  • Court Proceedings
    • 2.1: Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: jonyj1005" and "Vote: fDAs" are acceptable votes, while "Vote: rtg", "Vote relay400", "vote: RTG", "Vote:relay400", "Vote: RTG", and "Vote: relay400" (unless you want the depths of despair to reign down on you all) are not.
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: Don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no lynch to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No lynch".
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it will prevent me from modkilling you is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes in the game are on a player, that player will be lynched. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no lynch, then there will be no lynch. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.10: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.11: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person lynched (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Work Shift
    • 3.1: Each day will be 3 weeks long. The moderator will never extend or shorten the deadline.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will always be posted in EST.
    • 3.3: If the deadline is met and no person is lynched, it will be considered no lynch and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 3.4: Each twilight will be 24 hours long. Players may post during this time. It will end when the moderator posts so in thread.
    • 3.5: Each night will be 5 days long.
    • 3.6: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.7: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.8: Night will end when the moderator posts that it has in thread.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.11: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me.

  • Public Manners
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, people can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I, the one and only RTG, am the moderator of the Cop Shop.
    • 5.2: relay400 is my comoderator.
    • 5.3: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.4: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.5: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 24 hours, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.


    Command Headquarters
    Jailbreak! Jailbreak! Jailbreak! 15 prisoners have escaped. This couldn't have been done alone by the prisoners, this must have been an inside job. We have traitors among us, and it is your goal to bring these men to justice.


    Alive (10)
    Archimedes15 GeneralCobra19
    Charge
    SFGiantsFan
    jonyj1005 chess24
    BZ1
    Kagebaka math_turtle37
    SupaHotSuKa benjaminsz
    atomicpianowitch


    Dead (2)
    statisticslol
    TeddyYT Kbwuf


    Warnings



    Current Phase: Night 2

Click to reveal hidden text
Cop Code of Conduct
1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
1.2: Do not post at night.
1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
1.7: If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
1.8: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
1.9: In your first post, post something that would usually give you a warning in a usual game, but is not actually breaking any rules in this game. If I do not find that you have followed this rule correctly, you will not be able to perform any actions Night 1 (if you have any to perform).
Court Proceedings
2.1: Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: jonyj1005" and "Vote: fDAs" are acceptable votes, while "Vote: rtg", "Vote relay400", "vote: RTG", "Vote:relay400", "Vote: RTG", and "Vote: relay400" (unless you want the depths of despair to reign down on you all) are not.
2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: Don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
2.4: You may vote for no lynch to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No lynch".
2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it will prevent me from modkilling you is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes in the game are on a player, that player will be lynched. It will then immediately be twilight.
2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no lynch, then there will be no lynch. It will then be twilight.
2.9: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
2.10: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
2.11: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person lynched (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

Work Shift
3.1: Each day will be 3 weeks long. The moderator will never extend or shorten the deadline.
3.2: The deadlines will always be posted in EST.
3.3: If the deadline is met and no person is lynched, it will be considered no lynch and will proceed to be twilight.
3.4: Each twilight will be 24 hours long. Players may post during this time. It will end when the moderator posts so in thread.
3.5: Each night will be 5 days long.
3.6: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
3.7: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
3.8: Night will end when the moderator posts that it has in thread.
3.9: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
3.10: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
3.11: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me.

Public Manners
4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
4.3: Follow all of the rules.
4.4: Be mature.
4.5: For a minor rule infraction, people can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled. Please do not make me modkill you.

Moderators/Co-Moderators
5.1: I, the one and only RTG, am the moderator of the Cop Shop.
5.2: relay400 is my comoderator.
5.3: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
5.4: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
5.5: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 24 hours, the incorrect votecount will become official.
5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

Hello Mafia Players,
Based on the recent discussion in many of the Mafia Forum stickies, I think we need a new set of rules that is clear to all and will be best for the interests of the forum, its players, and its quality of games. I have discussed these rules with some fellow moderators (others did not respond to my PM in the last few days; hence, why this list is "tentative") and modified the rules accordingly.

These rules will be effective as soon as all players understand them and agree to them and we figure out exactly how many games each player has played in/playing in, etc. Note that if you are in violation of any rules at the moment (for example, playing in too many games), this is not a problem; just don't join any new games until these rules say you can.

To preserve the integrity of the Mafia forum, do not create a topic within it unless you have been directly authorized to do so by PM by a moderator of the forum. Any violator of this rule will be warned, and any repeat violators will be suspended from participating in Mafia games indefinitely.

Rules for SE/IC:
1) Any player with 3 or more games completed can apply to be an SE. Success is decided upon by forum mods and, to a lesser degree, the Mafia community.
2) Any SE with 5 or more games completed can apply to be an IC. Success is decided upon by forum mods, and to a lesser degree, the Mafia community. Note the success rate will be lower, as IC is much more meaningful of a title than SE.
3) Anyone who is not an SE or an IC will be referred to as a newbie, regardless of how many games they have completed.
Note: The terms "newbie", "SE", and "IC" should not be used in any manner for determining sign-up lists of non-newbie games (unlike the typical 6-2-1 arrangement in newbie games) but should be kept in mind due to the game limit: see Rules for Game Players below.

Definition of a Completed Game:
A game is considered "completed" by a player if:
1) The player is not replaced until the end of the game.
2) The player does not partake in activities/playstyles such as game-throwing, excessive fluff, etc.
3) The game is not player-abandoned.
Mod-abandoned games and replace-ins will be dealt with on a case-to-case basis. If you replace in early in a game, you will likely be considered as completing the game. (Example: me, in Mafia Game 57) If you replace late in the game, you will likely NOT be considered as completing the game. (No examples, but I'm sure you can find them) Mod-abandoned games in late-game states will likely be considered completed, mod-abandoned games in early-game states will likely NOT be considered completed BUT the game should be restarted with the same players and a better mod.

Note: "games completed" do not necessarily have to be on AoPS. If you send us (the moderators) a link to a forum (NOT chat or chat-style) game you have completed (such as one on mafiascum), and we see that your play is up to standards, that will count as a game completed. However, we would like to see you play in AoPS before granting SE/IC status.

Rules for Game Players:
1) A newbie with 0 completed games can only play in 1 game. That game must be a newbie game. Exceptions may be made on a case-to-case basis.
2) A newbie with 1 completed game can only play in 2 games: one newbie and one regular. If he/she only wants to play in one game, it can be any game.
3) A newbie with 2 or more completed games can play in any 2 games, but only 2 games.
4) A newbie cannot be in more than 1 newbie game at a time. This is designed to increase opportunities for newer players to play more often.
5) An SE can play in 3 games at once.
6) An IC can play in 4 games at once.
7) Do not discuss ongoing games without the permission of the moderator of that game.
8) Do not spam.
9) Obey all instructions given by the moderator of a game which you are participating in provided that the instructions are related to said game and do not violate any forum rules.

Rules for Game Mods/Comods:
1) All game mods must be SEs or ICs that have previously comodded a game.
2) All game comods must have at least 2 completed games.
3) If you mod-abandon a game or mod an unsuccessful game, you may not mod another game until you have successfully (actively, correctly, efficiently) comodded another game.

3.6: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
3.7: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
3.8: Night will end when the moderator posts that it has in thread.
3.9: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
3.10: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
3.11: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me.

Public Manners
4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
4.3: Follow all of the rules.
4.4: Be mature.
4.5: For a minor rule infraction, people can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled. Please do not make me modkill you.

Moderators/Co-Moderators
5.1: I, the one and only RTG, am the moderator of the Cop Shop.
5.2: relay400 is my comoderator.
5.3: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
5.4: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
5.5: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 24 hours, the incorrect votecount will become official.
5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

Hello Mafia Players,
Based on the recent discussion in many of the Mafia Forum stickies, I think we need a new set of rules that is clear to all and will be best for the interests of the forum, its players, and its quality of games. I have discussed these rules with some fellow moderators (others did not respond to my PM in the last few days; hence, why this list is "tentative") and modified the rules accordingly.

These rules will be effective as soon as all players understand them and agree to them and we figure out exactly how many games each player has played in/playing in, etc. Note that if you are in violation of any rules at the moment (for example, playing in too many games), this is not a problem; just don't join any new games until these rules say you can.

To preserve the integrity of the Mafia forum, do not create a topic within it unless you have been directly authorized to do so by PM by a moderator of the forum. Any violator of this rule will be warned, and any repeat violators will be suspended from participating in Mafia games indefinitely.

Rules for SE/IC:
1) Any player with 3 or more games completed can apply to be an SE. Success is decided upon by forum mods and, to a lesser degree, the Mafia community.
2) Any SE with 5 or more games completed can apply to be an IC. Success is decided upon by forum mods, and to a lesser degree, the Mafia community. Note the success rate will be lower, as IC is much more meaningful of a title than SE.
3) Anyone who is not an SE or an IC will be referred to as a newbie, regardless of how many games they have completed.
Note: The terms "newbie", "SE", and "IC" should not be used in any manner for determining sign-up lists of non-newbie games (unlike the typical 6-2-1 arrangement in newbie games) but should be kept in mind due to the game limit: see Rules for Game Players below.

Definition of a Completed Game:
A game is considered "completed" by a player if:
1) The player is not replaced until the end of the game.
2) The player does not partake in activities/playstyles such as game-throwing, excessive fluff, etc.
3) The game is not player-abandoned.
Mod-abandoned games and replace-ins will be dealt with on a case-to-case basis. If you replace in early in a game, you will likely be considered as completing the game. (Example: me, in Mafia Game 57) If you replace late in the game, you will likely NOT be considered as completing the game. (No examples, but I'm sure you can find them) Mod-abandoned games in late-game states will likely be considered completed, mod-abandoned games in early-game states will likely NOT be considered completed BUT the game should be restarted with the same players and a better mod.

Note: "games completed" do not necessarily have to be on AoPS. If you send us (the moderators) a link to a forum (NOT chat or chat-style) game you have completed (such as one on mafiascum), and we see that your play is up to standards, that will count as a game completed. However, we would like to see you play in AoPS before granting SE/IC status.

Rules for Game Players:
1) A newbie with 0 completed games can only play in 1 game. That game must be a newbie game. Exceptions may be made on a case-to-case basis.
2) A newbie with 1 completed game can only play in 2 games: one newbie and one regular. If he/she only wants to play in one game, it can be any game.
3) A newbie with 2 or more completed games can play in any 2 games, but only 2 games.
4) A newbie cannot be in more than 1 newbie game at a time. This is designed to increase opportunities for newer players to play more often.
5) An SE can play in 3 games at once.
6) An IC can play in 4 games at once.
7) Do not discuss ongoing games without the permission of the moderator of that game.
8) Do not spam.
9) Obey all instructions given by the moderator of a game which you are participating in provided that the instructions are related to said game and do not violate any forum rules.

Rules for Game Mods/Comods:
1) All game mods must be SEs or ICs that have previously comodded a game.
2) All game comods must have at least 2 completed games.
3) If you mod-abandon a game or mod an unsuccessful game, you may not mod another game until you have successfully (actively, correctly, efficiently) comodded another game.


Not in this game, no.

1.9: In your first post, post something that would usually give you a warning in a usual game, but is not actually breaking any rules in this game. If I do not find that you have followed this rule correctly, you will not be able to perform any actions Night 1 (if you have any to perform).

2.1: Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". 3.6: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
3.7: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
3.8: Night will end when the moderator posts that it has in thread.
3.9: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
3.10: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
3.11: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me.

Public Manners
4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
4.3: Follow all of the rules.
4.4: Be mature.
4.5: For a minor rule infraction, people can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled. Please do not make me modkill you.

Moderators/Co-Moderators
5.1: I, the one and only RTG, am the moderator of the Cop Shop.
5.2: relay400 is my comoderator.
5.3: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
5.4: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
5.5: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 24 hours, the incorrect votecount will become official.
5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

Hello Mafia Players,
Based on the recent discussion in many of the Mafia Forum stickies, I think we need a new set of rules that is clear to all and will be best for the interests of the forum, its players, and its quality of games. I have discussed these rules with some fellow moderators (others did not respond to my PM in the last few days; hence, why this list is "tentative") and modified the rules accordingly.

These rules will be effective as soon as all players understand them and agree to them and we figure out exactly how many games each player has played in/playing in, etc. Note that if you are in violation of any rules at the moment (for example, playing in too many games), this is not a problem; just don't join any new games until these rules say you can.

To preserve the integrity of the Mafia forum, do not create a topic within it unless you have been directly authorized to do so by PM by a moderator of the forum. Any violator of this rule will be warned, and any repeat violators will be suspended from participating in Mafia games indefinitely.

Rules for SE/IC:
1) Any player with 3 or more games completed can apply to be an SE. Success is decided upon by forum mods and, to a lesser degree, the Mafia community.
2) Any SE with 5 or more games completed can apply to be an IC. Success is decided upon by forum mods, and to a lesser degree, the Mafia community. Note the success rate will be lower, as IC is much more meaningful of a title than SE.
3) Anyone who is not an SE or an IC will be referred to as a newbie, regardless of how many games they have completed.
Note: The terms "newbie", "SE", and "IC" should not be used in any manner for determining sign-up lists of non-newbie games (unlike the typical 6-2-1 arrangement in newbie games) but should be kept in mind due to the game limit: see Rules for Game Players below.

Definition of a Completed Game:
A game is considered "completed" by a player if:
1) The player is not replaced until the end of the game.
2) The player does not partake in activities/playstyles such as game-throwing, excessive fluff, etc.
3) The game is not player-abandoned.
Mod-abandoned games and replace-ins will be dealt with on a case-to-case basis. If you replace in early in a game, you will likely be considered as completing the game. (Example: me, in Mafia Game 57) If you replace late in the game, you will likely NOT be considered as completing the game. (No examples, but I'm sure you can find them) Mod-abandoned games in late-game states will likely be considered completed, mod-abandoned games in early-game states will likely NOT be considered completed BUT the game should be restarted with the same players and a better mod.

Note: "games completed" do not necessarily have to be on AoPS. If you send us (the moderators) a link to a forum (NOT chat or chat-style) game you have completed (such as one on mafiascum), and we see that your play is up to standards, that will count as a game completed. However, we would like to see you play in AoPS before granting SE/IC status.

Rules for Game Players:
1) A newbie with 0 completed games can only play in 1 game. That game must be a newbie game. Exceptions may be made on a case-to-case basis.
2) A newbie with 1 completed game can only play in 2 games: one newbie and one regular. If he/she only wants to play in one game, it can be any game.
3) A newbie with 2 or more completed games can play in any 2 games, but only 2 games.
4) A newbie cannot be in more than 1 newbie game at a time. This is designed to increase opportunities for newer players to play more often.
5) An SE can play in 3 games at once.
6) An IC can play in 4 games at once.
7) Do not discuss ongoing games without the permission of the moderator of that game.
8) Do not spam.
9) Obey all instructions given by the moderator of a game which you are participating in provided that the instructions are related to said game and do not violate any forum rules.

Rules for Game Mods/Comods:
1) All game mods must be SEs or ICs that have previously comodded a game.
2) All game comods must have at least 2 completed games.
3) If you mod-abandon a game or mod an unsuccessful game, you may not mod another game until you have successfully (actively, correctly, efficiently) comodded another game.[/hide]

Em...so...

I think there's 3 or less scum. 4 seems too large of a number.
Which means after I die there should be at least 5 cops. (Yes, I'm assuming this is a setup with only cops & scum)
I say 4 people "investigate" one person, and 4 "investigate" the other (Teddy and jonyj because we shouldn't let pro scum slip)

Start of D3: 3 people on one person, 4 on the other
If all 4 people agree on a person's alignment, that is their alignment.
If all 3 people agree on a person's alignment (town), then that person is town.
If all 3 people agree on a person's alignment (scum), then that person is NOT necessarily scum. If he is, that does NOT mean the 3 people who said he was are all town.
If there are mixed answers:
Ex: 2 people say A is scum, the other says A is town
Lynch the scummiest of the 3. If they turn scum, whoever agreed with them (on result) is also scum. BUT, the other is NOT confirmed town.
Ex: 1 person says B is scum, other 3 say B is town
Lynch the person that said B is scum. Then what's next is obvious.

Of course this also relies on the fact that D3 isn't Lynch-or-Lose. (<3 scum, which may or may not be obvious)
And some people might investigate other people...

Of course, this is up to you guys to have an unanimous vote that says to implement this plan, despite its limitations (and well, scum will have time to react because I've just posted it.)

RTG wrote:

However, since you spend most of your day with the prisoners, you don't have much time for political conversations that they have upstairs in the rest of the station.

Each night you may target one player. This player gets protected from all other night actions used on them, but also cannot perform a night action. You are also limited to posting only 10 times per day. You also must not discuss your post restriction. If you break these rules, you will lose your night action for one night, and if you continue to break these rules, you may get force replaced. You also have the power of your say and your vote. Each night you may target one player. You get to receive whether they are innocent or guilty. You also have the power of your say and your vote. You are town aligned.
